updates.title: Google AI Mode Now Shows Shopping Ads and Sponsored Stores
updates.summary: Google AI Mode, with 75M+ daily active users, now supports shopping ad formats and sponsored stores inside conversational search. Advertisers using PMax, Shopping, and Search broad match (including AI Max) are automatically eligible.
updates.action: Ensure you are running PMax or Shopping campaigns with broad match to appear in AI Mode. No separate opt-in needed.

updates.title: Performance Max Gets First-Party Audience Exclusions and Budget Reporting
updates.summary: PMax now lets you exclude specific customer lists, add budget forecasting reports inside campaigns, and view placement reports segmented by network. Expanded audience reporting with age/gender breakdowns also added.
updates.action: Exclude existing customers from PMax to focus on acquisition. Check the new budget report tab to forecast end-of-month spend.

updates.title: VRC Non-Skip Ads Go Global for Connected TV
updates.summary: VRC Non-Skip ads are now generally available globally in Google Ads and DV360. AI optimizes across 6-second bumpers, 15-second standard, and 30-second CTV-only non-skippable formats for living room YouTube inventory.
updates.action: Test VRC Non-Skip for CTV campaigns targeting living room viewers. Let AI optimize across the three format lengths.

updates.title: Google Ads Editor 2.12: 15 New Features Including PMax Video Limit Increase
updates.summary: Editor 2.12 adds 15 features spanning PMax, Demand Gen, Search, Shopping, and cross-account management. PMax asset groups now support up to 15 videos (up from previous limit).
updates.action: Update to Editor 2.12. Add more video assets to PMax asset groups to give the algorithm more creative options.

updates.title: Performance Planner Drops Display and Video Campaign Support
updates.summary: As of March 9, Performance Planner no longer supports Display and Video campaigns. Google is repositioning it around conversion-driven types: Search, Shopping, App, Demand Gen, Local, and PMax.
updates.action: Stop using Performance Planner for Display/Video forecasting. Use it only for Search, Shopping, and PMax planning.

updates.title: Demand Gen Gets AI Video Generation from Images and Creator Partnerships
updates.summary: Google now lets advertisers use Veo AI to generate video content from static images for Demand Gen campaigns. New creator partnership discovery tool lets you find YouTube creators and turn their content into ads.
updates.action: Test AI video generation from your best-performing product images. Explore the creator partnership tool for authentic ad content.

updates.title: Google Ads Editor 2.9 and Earlier No Longer Supported After March 30
updates.summary: Google is ending support for Editor versions 2.9 and earlier. Users must update to continue managing campaigns through the desktop tool.
updates.action: Update Google Ads Editor to version 2.12 before March 30.

updates.title: Meta Deploys Major AI Delivery System Update: Outcome-Based Optimization
updates.summary: In the first week of March, Meta moved from auction-based placement optimization to outcome-based optimization. The system now predicts downstream conversions rather than optimizing for intermediate engagement metrics.
updates.action: Expect performance fluctuations as the new system learns. Give campaigns 7-10 days before making changes. Focus on conversion goals, not engagement metrics.

updates.title: Meta Click Attribution Now Counts Only Link Clicks, Not All Interactions
updates.summary: Click-through attribution now counts only actual link clicks. Non-link interactions (likes, comments, shares) are categorized as engage-through attribution. Combined with engaged-view dropping from 10s to 5s, reported conversions will look different.
updates.action: Re-baseline your conversion expectations. Compare new attribution numbers against CRM data to understand the real delta. Do not panic-cut budgets based on the metric shift.

updates.title: Meta Ad Policy Crackdown: 34% More Rejections in Health/Wellness/Beauty
updates.summary: Meta upgraded its multimodal AI enforcement in Q1 2026 with new rules on misleading claims, personal attributes detection, crypto ad tiers, and blood sugar/diabetes claim restrictions. Rejection rates spiked 34% for health, wellness, and beauty verticals.
updates.action: Audit all ad copy in health/wellness/beauty for claims compliance. Remove before/after language, implied diagnoses, and unsubstantiated health claims before they get flagged.

updates.title: WhatsApp Status Ads Auto-Activate in Traffic Campaigns
updates.summary: WhatsApp Status ad placement is expanding and now activates automatically in Traffic campaigns optimized for link clicks, daily reach, or impressions when using Advantage+ placements.
updates.action: Check your Traffic campaigns for WhatsApp Status placement. Review performance separately. Opt out via placement exclusion if it does not convert for your market.

updates.title: Advantage+ Threshold Drops to 25 Conversions/Week, Gets Predictive Budgets
updates.summary: Advantage+ Sales now requires only 25 conversions per week to qualify, down from 50. New Predictive Budget Allocation dynamically shifts spend based on predicted conversion probability, reporting 8-15% ROAS improvement.
updates.action: Re-evaluate Advantage+ for accounts that previously did not hit the 50 conversion threshold. Enable Predictive Budget Allocation and test for 2 weeks.

updates.title: Describe Your Audience: Natural Language Targeting Inside Advantage+
updates.summary: Meta added a plain-text box inside Advantage+ Targeting where you write a description of your ideal customer instead of selecting interest categories. Meta AI interprets the description and builds the targeting.
updates.action: Test natural language targeting on one campaign. Describe your ICP in 2-3 sentences. Compare reach and conversion quality against interest-based targeting.

updates.title: Meta Location Fees (Digital Service Tax) Start April 2026
updates.summary: Meta will add Location Fees to cover Digital Service Taxes starting April 2026. Rates: Austria 5%, France 3%, Italy 3%, Spain 3%, Turkey 5%, UK 2%. This increases effective ad costs in those markets.
updates.action: Factor Location Fees into budget planning for EU/UK campaigns. Adjust CPA targets upward by the fee percentage for affected countries.

updates.title: TikTok NewFronts 2026: 5 New Premium Ad Formats Including Logo Takeover
updates.summary: TikTok unveiled Logo Takeover (brand logo on splash screen), Prime Time (3 sequential ads in 15-min window), TopReach (TopView + TopFeed combined), Pulse Mentions (ads next to brand mentions), and Pulse Tastemakers (ads alongside curated creators).
updates.action: Consider Logo Takeover or TopReach for product launches. Test Pulse Mentions to appear next to organic brand conversations. These are premium/high-visibility buys.

updates.title: TikTok Launches Engaged Session: Pixel-Free Post-Click Tracking
updates.summary: New Engaged Session tool tracks users who spend 10+ seconds on a site after clicking an ad, without requiring a pixel. Provides Total Engaged Sessions and Cost per Engaged Session metrics. Available under Traffic objective.
updates.action: Enable Engaged Session on Traffic campaigns as a supplementary metric. Useful for advertisers who cannot install the TikTok pixel on their site.

updates.title: ChatGPT Enters Advertising via Programmatic Partnership
updates.summary: ChatGPT formally entered the advertising market through a programmatic partnership in March 2026. This opens a new channel for advertisers to reach users inside conversational AI interfaces.
updates.action: Monitor ChatGPT ad beta availability. Start thinking about how your brand shows up in AI conversations. Ensure your website content is structured for AI citation.

updates.title: AI Overviews Crash Paid CTR from 19.7% to 6.34%
updates.summary: Studies show paid CTR on queries with AI Overviews dropped from 19.7% to 6.34%. As click volume decreases, CPC rises due to fewer available clicks and increased competition. Zero-click searches are accelerating.
updates.action: Diversify beyond search. Invest in brand building, email, and direct traffic. For SEO, optimize to be cited BY AI Overviews. For paid, expect higher CPCs on AI Overview queries.

updates.title: Google March 2026 Core Update Causes Extreme SEO Volatility
updates.summary: Google launched its March 2026 core update alongside a spam update. Semrush Sensor reported Very High keyword volatility for nearly the entire month. Major ranking shifts across all verticals.
updates.action: Do not make reactive SEO changes during the update. Monitor rankings for 2-3 weeks post-rollout. Focus on content quality and E-E-A-T signals.

updates.title: HubSpot: Search Is No Longer the Starting Point for Most Buyer Journeys
updates.summary: HubSpot 2026 State of Marketing report (1,500+ marketers) found buyers now move through AI tools, social feeds, creator content, communities, and review sites before reaching a brand website. Search is one touchpoint, not the first one.
updates.action: Map your buyer journey across AI tools, social, review sites, and communities. Invest in presence across all these touchpoints, not just search.

updates.title: WPP Dissolves Holding Company Model: Merges Ogilvy, VML, AKQA
updates.summary: WPP announced Elevate28, dissolving its holding company model and consolidating Ogilvy, VML, AKQA, and WPP Media under a unified structure. Targeting 500M GBP in annualized savings by 2028.
updates.action: If working with any WPP agency, expect structural changes. This signals a broader industry shift toward consolidated, AI-driven agency operations.
